title: Maxillaria crinita
taxonomy: Maxillaria crinita is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. This family is further divided into approximately 763 genera, with Maxillaria being one of them. Maxillaria crinita is specifically placed in the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is a large and widespread subfamily within the Orchidaceae family. The species itself is also grouped within the tribe Maxillarieae, which includes approximately 700 species of orchids. Maxillaria crinita is a member of the genus Maxillaria, which consists of around 400 species of orchids.

intro: Maxillaria crinita, also known as the "Hairy Maxillaria" or "Crinita Orchid," is a species of flowering orchid native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Maxillaria crinita include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Maxillaria crinita include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
